mentions.note: Don't take the selling personally; Cramer loves the stock and says own it, don't trade it. Selling driven by speculators raising cash for SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI, not fundamental weakness.
Major source of liquidity for IPO speculators. Selling is not a negative on the stock itself. Own it, don't trade it. Much IPO capital will eventually buy Nvidia equipment.
Like Apple, selling driven by speculators cashing in for IPO participation, not fundamental weakness. Cramer: own it, don't trade it. Will benefit from SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI capex.

Same forced selling as Apple for SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI fundraising. Cramer loves Nvidia; own it, don't trade it. Will be a primary beneficiary of mega-IPO proceeds (used for AI chip purchases). Ignore near-term selling pressure.

Massive selling source for SpaceX IPO funding. Excellent company fundamentally; selling is forced liquidation. Own it, don't trade it. Will likely benefit when IPO capital redeploys into AI infrastructure.
Only mega-cap tech with pristine buyback and balance sheet; will benefit from all data center IPOs; being slaughtered unfairly and will snap back first out of the tech mass.

Market linchpin; power behind generative AI, agents, training. Sovereign AI adoption (South Korea, Singapore, India, Japan, etc.) now 14% of revenue and growing, reducing hyperscaler-defection risk. Google paying $920M/month to SpaceX for Nvidia GPUs proves customer preference for Nvidia over in-house chips. Own it long-term, don't trade. Near-term IPO wave will pressure valuations, but Nvidia's strategic moat is intact.
Stock playing shoots-and-ladders with shareholders; Goldman Sachs mojo absent; lots of unknown risks despite chip dominance; no clear catalyst.

Excellent AI story and Jensen Huang leadership remain intact, but stock likely to be 'market donor' as buyers liquidate to fund mega-cap IPOs. Down $8 today (-3.62%); too easy to sell. Expect continued pressure until mega-raises conclude.
Jensen Wong's endorsements of data center customers irresistible; more chip spending = more profits. Own it, don't trade it.

Computex keynote delivered transformational thesis: compute is money, data center economics are viable. RTX Spark Superchip and Vera Rubin establish dominance; stock rallied 6% on validated vision. Own it, don't trade it.

Inexplicable $3 decline on no news despite being world's largest stock; cryptic 'new era of PC' tweet hints at Computex unveiling Sunday night. Still fundamentally sound but needs clarity on mystery selloff.

Largest company in world; reported fabulous quarter but stock went down; traded lower on earnings last 4 quarters; only up 15% YTD despite best AI tech; needs cash management pivot—more dividends and buybacks à la Apple under Maestri; may need to trim portfolio winners for additional firepower.
Consistently expensive on near-term P/E but cheap on forward out-years due to CEO Jensen Huang's relentless outperformance; own it don't trade it; up 800%+ in 2023-24.
Fantastic long-term story with most powerful semiconductors for AI applications; insanely volatile but core position trading method works well; own it don't trade it.

Q1 crushing with 85% YoY revenue growth to $81.6B, beat by $3B, $10B free cash flow beat; guiding Q2 $91B (+$4B above consensus); $80B new buyback announced. Stock flat despite beat reflects $5T market cap saturation, not fundamentals. Own it, don't trade it.
Magnificent Seven member; AI chip leader with secular growth opportunity; rate-proof and scalable.
Data center beating heart; own don't trade from single digits through $200 doldrums to $225 now; need perfect quarter to go much higher but likely to get it.

Up 4.4% today on valuation thesis, not China reopening. Trading 26x sales—CHEAPER than S&P 500 average despite 65% revenue growth. Below cost of Cerebras (187x), AMD (21x), Broadcom (33x) on comparable basis. Now offers asymmetric risk/reward as absurdly cheap large-cap semiconductor with AI dominance.
One of two stocks earning 'own it, don't trade it' highest blessing; revolutionary company with outstanding management; AI leadership validated by Jensen Huang's credibility.

Founder Jensen Huang leading fourth industrial revolution (AI/accelerated computing). Core thesis intact; P/E multiples justified by infrastructure buildout scale and supply chain dominance.

Pioneer of AI/compute revolution; still largest company on earth. Stock feels left behind despite foundational role, but remains core to entire ecosystem. Own it.
